old_claim.note: Logarithms & Early Calculus is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No separate logarithms-and-calculus edge remains because the broader sourced mathematics edge already represents quantitative capability without asserting one bundled technique as the unique lineage.

source_shape.source_locator: NASA presents quantitative force, pressure, lift, and drag relationships but does not identify the graph's bundled Logarithms and Early Calculus node as a direct historical prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports mathematics broadly as an aerodynamic capability without pinning a unique logarithms-calculus lineage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping both mathematics and a narrower bundled mathematics node duplicates the same context and overstates a specific lineage not licensed by the cited history.

ontology_before: Aerodynamics carried both a general mathematics edge and an unsourced bundled logarithms-calculus predecessor.
ontology_after: The sourced mathematics edge carries quantitative capability without a redundant specific mathematical lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: logarithms_calculus_early is no longer a direct prerequisite for aerodynamics_subsonic.
would_reject_if: A source explicitly tied the earliest scoped aerodynamic formulation to a named logarithmic or calculus method represented by that node.
The broad mathematics edge were removed and a more precise calculus dependency became necessary to preserve the mechanism.
short_reason: The specific bundled math edge duplicated broader sourced mathematics.
